data.title: WLFI reserve company ALT5 Sigma will be penalized for violating SEC regulations by failing to promptly disclose the suspension of its senior executives.
data.description: WLFI reserve firm ALT5 Sigma stated in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) that its CEO was officially suspended on October 16. However, internal emails show that the company's board of directors had actually placed him on "temporary leave" as early as September 4. Several securities regulatory experts stated that this significant discrepancy in timing may violate disclosure rules. According to SEC regulations, publicly traded companies must disclose such a significant change in the actual cessation of an executive's duties within four trading days (Form 8-K). If a company intentionally submits false or misleading information, it may constitute a violation of anti-fraud regulations. (Forbes)

data.title: Goldman Sachs' Tony Pasquariello: Signs of Bull Market Fatigue in U.S. Stock Market
data.description: According to Blockbeats, Goldman Sachs partner Tony Pasquariello believes that the U.S. stock market has shown signs of a bullish surrender, and further selling pressure is expected before the market stabilizes. Although Nvidia announced impressive earnings beats and raised guidance, the market seems to be entering a new phase of the artificial intelligence cycle, with investors becoming skeptical about the sustainability of capital expenditures and future returns of mega-scale cloud service providers.

data.title: Crypto Biz: Is this what a bear market looks like? (Spoiler: No)
data.description:
Bitcoin's price dips below $90,000, yet Republic and Kraken ink nine-figure deals. If this is a bear market, someone forgot to tell big money.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-biz-is-this-bear-market-spoiler-no?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss%3F_ts%3D1763741726869%26cachebust%3Dtrue%26_%3D1763741726869%26timestamp%3D1763741726869&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Fanatics disclosed that its prediction market is expected to launch in the coming weeks.
data.description: Fanatics CEO Michael Rubin stated that the company is entering the prediction market business through a partnership with crypto platform Crypto.com. The new platform is expected to launch in the coming weeks, but no further details regarding its specific form or operational method have been revealed. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Eli Lily Hits $1 trillion Market cap With Its Weight Loss Injection
data.description:

data.title: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics announced that the October CPI report will not be released; the November report will be released on December 18.
data.description: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics announced that the October Consumer Price Index (CPI) report will not be released; the November CPI report will be released on December 18. (Jinshi)

data.title: CZ: I may have played a role in influencing the Bridgewater Associates founder's decision to purchase Bitcoin.
data.description: CZ posted on the X platform, saying, "I may have played a role in Bridgewater Associates founder Ray Dalio's allocation of Bitcoin purchases, and of course, in exchange, I also learned more from him." Previously, it was reported that Ray Dalio disclosed he has long held a small amount of Bitcoin, which has consistently accounted for approximately 1% of his portfolio.

data.title: Adding DeFi to your 401k: How BlackRock’s staked Ethereum ETF rewires access to ETH rewards
data.description:
BlackRock registered the iShares Staked Ethereum Trust in Delaware on Nov. 19, opening a path toward the firm’s first staked Ethereum ETF in the US. The state-level trust registration does not constitute a formal Securities Act of 1933 application
source: https://cryptoslate.com/adding-defi-to-your-401k-how-blackrocks-staked-ethereum-etf-rewires-access-to-eth-yield/

data.title: Winter Is Coming: Crypto Season Slides Into Dormant Phase as Bitcoin and Altcoins Retreat
data.description:
Fear is dominating today’s trading across the crypto market as the CMC Crypto Fear and Greed Index sits at 11, the lowest reading since its launch, while Bitcoin is trading around $83,000 after retreating sharply from recent six-figure levels. Market data and recent commentary attribute the slide to heavy liquidations, fading inflows into spot products, and […]
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/winter-is-coming-crypto-season-slides-into-dormant-phase-as-bitcoin-and-altcoins-retreat/

data.title: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq indices once hit new lows in more than two months.
data.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq indices both hit new lows in more than two months. The Nasdaq opened higher but then fell today. Nvidia (NVDA.O) is currently down 1.47%, and Oracle (ORCL.N) is down 5.7% to a five-month low. (Jinshi)

data.title: Over $55 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network in the past hour, including $37.08 million in BTC liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $55.3424 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, mainly long positions. Among them, $37.08 million in BTC and $10.13 million in ETH were liquidated.

data.title: GOOG Stock Rises Ahead of Google’s Final Court Plea in Ad-Tech Breakup Case
data.description:
